title: Project Report: Incident Response Tabletop Exercise — IT Support / Helpdesk
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Simulate ransomware incident: detect, contain, eradicate, recover, and write post-incident report.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**Incident Response Tabletop Exercise** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for IT Support / Helpdesk. Duration: 3–4 weeks. Simulate ransomware incident: detect, contain, eradicate, recover, and write post-incident report. Tools: NIST IR framework, ITIL.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Incident response plan document
Objective: Tabletop exercise with timeline
Objective: Containment playbook
Objective: Post-incident report with lessons learned
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
IT Support / Helpdesk portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a IT Support / Helpdesk resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a IT Support / Helpdesk resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Plan
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Plan** *(Level: Advanced)*

IR plan document. Complete these tasks: Roles; Escalation; Contacts. Deliverable for this phase: IR plan PDF.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Roles
- [ ] Escalation
- [ ] Contacts
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

Phase Plan is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### Where to use it

### Real use example

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: IR plan; Exercise timeline; PIR document; Updated playbook. Skills demonstrated: Incident response, Security, Documentation. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: IR plan
Deliverable: Exercise timeline
Deliverable: PIR document
Deliverable: Updated playbook
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits IR plan and passes the rubric.
